name: Archon1
description: Archon1 is an archaerhodopsin-3-derived genetically encoded voltage indicator developed via robotic directed evolution, achieving a 2.4-fold brightness increase over its template (QuasAr2) with millisecond kinetics and high photostability. It enables imaging of action potentials and millivolt-scale subthreshold events in neurons, brain slices, zebrafish, and C. elegans.

researchPapers.title: A robotic multidimensional directed evolution approach applied to fluorescent voltage reporters
researchPapers.authors: Piatkevich KD, Jung EE, Straub C, Linghu C, Park D, Suk HJ, Hochbaum DR, Goodwin D, Pnevmatikakis E, Pak N, Kawashima T, Yang CT, Rhoades JL, Shemesh O, Asano S, Yoon YG, Freifeld L, Saulnier JL, Riegler C, Engert F, Hughes T, Drobizhev M, Bhatt DL, Boyden ES
researchPapers.sample: Cultured neurons, Acute brain slices, Zebrafish larvae, C. elegans

researchPapers.title: In Vitro Testing of Voltage Indicators: Archon1, ArcLightD, ASAP1, ASAP2s, ASAP3b, Bongwoori-Pos6, BeRST1, FlicR1, and Chi-VSFP-Butterfly

researchPapers.title: Voltage Imaging of Cardiac Cells and Tissue Using the Genetically Encoded Voltage Sensor Archon1
